What that means is it makes Perfect Sense That's what an airtight logical case does no ifs it just makes sense So the mistake That most sales people make when they go about doing this Is they don't spend time Walking in the customer's shoes They don't do that for that backwards looking thing where you say, okay, if I was that person Based on where they are in life right now based on what their business is with what their position is What would they want to hear? logically To be able to make the right decision. Okay, I'll give you an example the trap is That you base Your airtight logical case In terms of that your product is really fairly priced Because price is a big thing right price is a very price is part of your listen It's always going to come down partly the price because we have what's called a cost Benefit ratio And you have a value proposition What these things are essentially a cost benefit is in other words based on the price I have to pay And the benefits I'm going to get well my cost is going to be you know $10 and my benefits at worth $100 I have 10 to 1 ratio. That's a very good cost benefit ratio right Conversely I'm using very simple numbers here conversely if the cost of my I'm charging a hundred dollars or something and value This is perceived value to the prospect is going to be 75 dollars That's not a very good cause it's a negative cost benefit ratio. It's not going to tip in your favor So one of the things that when I'm Making an airtight logical case. I'm always focusing in on the cost benefit ratio Essentially, what am I going to charge this person for the product? good or service and based on that What are the actual tangible? benefits to this person dollar wise Those what what what can they make off of my product now for instance when I did this training a few weeks back for this company that was in the digital digital marketing company, right? Then the fatal mistake they were making they're still doing well by the way you're with it is they were trying to explain their product and I'll just put I'm going to put an arbitrary value at a thousand dollars thousand dollars spent right When they were trying to explain to their prospects Why the product was priced at a thousand dollars? And they stacked them all this and now and here's the price for the program And they say the price is x dollars and let me tell you why we price it that way Because it takes us x number of man hours. We had to write all the code And I'm like That's not that's not an airtight logical case for anything other than I'm not ripping you off That's all that accomplishes that I'm not overcharge you Doesn't mean you should why would you want to I mean, okay, here's the good news The cost-benefit ratio sucks, but I'm not overcharge you the price just not very valuable Like that's what that that's what it says those the fact that you're not making a lot of money on it Doesn't mean that it has any benefit to the prospect That's not an airtight logical case an airtight logical case is this Okay, I'm going to bring you in Okay, I have four different Features four different things that we do parts of the program This part of the program and I'll use I'll use digital marketing We work on your actual landing page We give you a perfect landing page that's structured just the right way with the phone number the call to action The graphics it's so cool that we know that when you have this landing page If you drive traffic there you'll get at least 50 percent more calls than the current page you have Because of these glaring errors on your current page and we know from experience that the by-law metrics here that if we drive 100 calls to this page The current structure will give you 50 hours gives you a 70 run make sense so far the guy says yeah Okay, because you explain that's all you know Because it's this feature that feature because of those features Okay, you're gonna get 50 percent higher conversion For the people that land on this page because it's structured more effectively and that's been proven out Time and time again make sense. Like just imagine, you know, you know, it's even an emotional case You don't have to consider all the different spaces. You can say just imagine Six months from now sitting back in your chair Would your business just put it fucking just spitting out money? You just feel so great about it. Honestly, that's emotional You don't have to connect the dots for that The problem though is that if you don't first connect up the dots And you try to just say that to someone they're like, oh, yeah bullshit bullshit The human mind has a bullshit detector because there's no logic involved So we always want to have the logical underpinning that we create And by doing that It opens someone up to be moved emotionally Let me give you a whole different perspective on this right for answers back and we're going to change like doing another way you do it, right? And in the stock market you have what's called a price Earnings ratio, which means that based on certain interest like consumer products might trade 20 times earnings You might have um, the high tech trades 30 times earnings The banks trade at 10 times everyone trades a certain multiple times this number So back in the days you'd say the entire Consumer products industry trades at 25 times start earnings. Now you'll accept it at face value because it makes sense, right? But That's a lot see logical case watch Logical case is and this is this is really where the road meets the road. So really listen closely on the logical case is that When companies that are facing major litigation when it first happens people bail out The stock drops because they're scared, right? They're forced to bail out And I give someone a very powerful tidbit. They're like, oh fuck. I don't know that one Those see what happens the reason it happens is no more people get scared But also companies large hedge funds banks. They have restrictive covenants They have sort of risk parameters that bar them from getting involved in companies facing major litigation That's why when these big companies get hit the stocks plummet And then predictably as soon as these companies set a litigation the funds that the restrictions of they all pour back Because they gotta by having the problem they pour back in the stocks fly up And they go back up even higher than they were that makes they guys. So now for instance, he has an example I'd say you I'll get you to the front page of google, right? Okay, kind of like the how logic is a why is that so crucial and without dragging on A little tidbit of just how we do that One of the true beauties what we do here sir is that we have people employed that are experts in this area and through strategic But and whatever it might doesn't matter what is I come with a boom a couple of words Okay, it allows you to pop up to page one and because of that Buying habits are that when someone goes in on the smartphone or home on the desktop Okay, they typically look at the first seven statistics say the first seven things they stop And you fill in the blanks there why it's important The airtight logical case is about the why so it's not so much like your your first case Is that your so you frame a case right you frame a logical case? And it's about one thing and then when you Build an airtight case. It's just more it's separate things but just more logic. That's not what it is It's the same thing that you started with but a deeper understanding of it. And that's where most people fall down They're not building a picture for someone They jump because just more arcane facts on top of more arcane facts and after a while people just say it's just they're overwhelmed versus allowing people to develop a very detailed understanding of why something is a good deal essentially of how So you know how walls but not but not to a point you're giving them a crazy words that just you know are flying over the heads But it's sort of the how and why of why something is truly a great deal Why it makes perfect empirical sense and you say it to them in a way when you're using in some industry jargon But you're not talking over someone's head See the last thing you want to do is Talk to someone in a series of techno babble and they don't understand what you're saying like I don't get it What you want to do the really smart people are able of great story tales are able to explain something to someone In a way that sounds very sophisticated But it's actually very easy to understand So when you're creating your scripts And you're developing your language patterns You want to use just enough technical industry jargon That you sound like you know, you know, you know, you're an expert in your field But not so much That people like what the fuck is this guy talking about? I don't get it. It's just like it's no old man and they tune out They almost like almost offended by they feel you trying to cover up something or you simply just not an effective communicate it and they don't buy from you You get it? So you want to use the power Of language to speak just about their head Like, you know, if someone like watch imagine a scale, you know, it's always nice to have a video, right? So imagine this is a one A one year and this is a 10 year Right and this is like when you speak in layman's terms Okay, we're like, you know, totally novice, right? This is where an educated person would be able to understand, right? And this is where an expert their language you want to be right around here like a seven Above the level that someone like a small person you want to be a little bit above that but not over here This is techno wizardry. It's bullshit and people tune out That's also part of developing an airtight logical case. Now remember, okay? One of the keys here is we we need to stack That's the word I want you to think about when you are creating your scripts, okay? Remember that you're the idea is that you want to have sort of three bites at the apple You're not gonna look to write this perfect Undeniablely logical pattern that's so great That when you're done the prospects like fuck that was so great I I just see no reason not to buy It doesn't work that way no matter what you do But how hard you try to create that level of certainty it doesn't happen all at once in one shot It takes more okay, but I gotta shut this fire guys one second Doesn't work so what you do Is you you want to sort of take three? Bites at the apple so you want to have these three Sort of you know they make up your first one is the main body of your presentation Your second bite is represented by your first loop And your third bite is represented by the next loop. So watch how it plays out here on the straight line. So watch Open Close this is your perfect sale right here These are your boundaries above and below the line You have your first four seconds where you capture their attention positions up as an expert you want to gather intelligence Right gi Build massive rapport. You do your straight line transition, right? And now you're gonna start by framing this logical case That goes to about here So in other words, you're still a lot of you're not like halfway down. You're still a lot of this is the frame And again, the best metaphor is look at the frame of the house. You lay the foundation You put up the the uh the overall the wood frame But you don't put up the drywall the like the the the electricity the plumbing that comes later So what you gotta do is when you when you you could write Your whole when you're building the script you can write the whole thing out in one shot But then you gotta look at okay. What am I dividing points here? What am I what's what's my best most logical way? So I can you know say x and then ask for the order for this is a watch so you want to have your initial language pattern Takes you to here And then you're gonna ask for the order for the first line. This is your first close right here So give me one shot and believe me You'll be very very impressed sound fair enough. That's right And then you're gonna either close the deal but more likely you'll get hit with your first objection, right? He says let me think about it So he goes off the line here. So you say I kill what is your stop them say I hear what you're saying But let me ask you a question Does the idea make sense to you do you like the idea right and he says yeah, it sounds pretty good And now you're gonna oh such a loop back On the line into presentation mode and you pick up At the exact point that you left off In your initial presentation. This is the key here these these presentations need to connect to one another Your first your initial presentation and your first looping pattern. Okay in the morgue and you know every industry Has its own sort of mechanism that people create airtight logical cases in mortgages It's the basically the life of loan the best one's the life LL why not let a life of loan savings in other words when you refinance refinance somebody's home Okay, you can take them from a long-term mortgage like 30 years down to 15 years And what that does if then if the rates drop that payment might go up a little tiny bit or not at all, right? But they save 12 years of payments And because of the way compound interest works it turns to be hundreds of thousands of dollars in savings Very attractive an inaudible case. Here's another way you do it, right? See to get them to buy I have to move them emotionally But I can't move them emotionally until I first get them logically certain When I get someone logically certain it frees them up to be moved emotionally That's the bottom line That's the secret and it's going to come down to the why so here's my signature So let's go back to the beginning why when I said in the very beginning of the training I said, okay There's two ways to go about this right if you're Part of a group, okay If in a group if it comes in group effort, right? Everybody submits Yeah, five core Reasons why it's impeccably logical Everyone puts them in a box of a hat, right? And then you start rifling through them all and you organize them in terms of like What's the most logical you start putting them into sequence? All right, what is a way to tell a story? Big picture to small picture, you know, most important at least important right the fine details come less But you put them into sequence, right and you divide them up over Three presentations Each presentation should have no more than three core points to it any more than that it becomes noise You get it so five people you have a bunch you get to pull out 15 of them You pull out nine of them, right and you use those nine as your core points Then you have some others you can use afterwards as backups, right? If you'll buy yourself you're a lone warrior No problem, okay Sit down, okay You can even do it over a couple of days You write a few down take a break come back right a few more down give yourself some time Extract everything out of you go to people in you're probably of other people you can go to look online Seriously, I it's by the way, I'm High by a company. I'll do read I'll go online. I'll see what other people are saying I look at landing page. What are the key benefits everyone's making? Very seldom that you can't find out everything you need to know by one little You know perusal online The information is out there Then once you have that what do you do you divide it up into Three chunks your three presentations now again on top of that There's then all the other rules of straight line script writing So the script's gotta you know, it has to meet all these other criteria But that's not what this you know, we there's other trains just you know punching in the search box You know scripting and you'll see other intensified then that go through the actual rules of scripting separate issue This is simply about what it really means To build an airtight logical case And without it It's really fucking hot to close it really is Because people when it does it poisons the second 10 in the third 10 You understand it's like and there's one so one's one someone's really truly logically certain It's the lubricant for everything else That's the way it really works because they start to think I make so much sense. You're you're on the level You're on the level it makes sense Once they think that The rest becomes pretty easy All right now now in the real let's not remember this okay in the real here's my one final thought here because it's very important Right in the real world of selling now While you only have to have three patterns it actually behooves you to have more like five Because remember not all customers will create equal They have different needs different beliefs different values different pain points So I want you to have some extra backup patterns as well For when the sale drags on because if at any point along the way You're running out of intelligence things to say it's not saying stupid shit You break reports over so you need to have your backup patterns as well Three core patterns you have three patterns for product Three patterns for yourself Three patterns for your company and those three patterns are designed as structured to build upon each other But you want to have your backups as well and also if you're in you know, it's like I guess it's like The more the merrier but only if you need them In other words, you want to have these reserve patterns here that sound great that makes sense What you don't want to do is under prepare yourself And get caught saying stupid shit I'll go back to that sale that I made that day in what it was a mock sale right where I was being filmed right The ability what would close that deal Was my ability to keep talking and talking and talking without putting my foot in the mouth I had already I kind of got the guy 15 or 18 minutes in the last four minutes was sort of my ability to keep going and getting using that You know to really kind of box them in but without Doing something that would sort of say ah, he's full of shit That's why you need to build the goal a bit lower. So you want to have your extra patterns in there just to safety's sake But really in reality those three patterns 80 90s and some those will do the trick Now remember logical certainty opens up the possibility And emotional certainty is what closes the prospect and emotional certainty is based on two things number one The minor part is your ability to use tonality To get someone excited So through the process of pace pace lead by you know, you becoming more certain to your tonality. They feel more certain as well That's far less powerful than the real way, which is through future pacing You allow someone To have the benefit now of imagining themselves in the future Using your product and feeling good That's emotional So, you know, they a car they're driving down the road Winding their hair top down feeling sexy feeling powerful loving life That's emotional certainty Imagine down the road having your broker so when you can trust build wealth over the long term and you ever tied feeling great Knowing your money's working for you. That's emotional certainty knowing that your money's working for you making your money That's not going to work well until you believe it's so like believing it's so is logical certainty And imagining what it's like is emotional. So you get the difference believing. It's going to be real is logical Enjoying what it looks like is emotional Two together fucking power Bottom line So that's the best I can do for you without actually having a specific Pattern to write like we know and probably we'll do that one day Well, actually, I'll I'll show you how I actually do one of you'll send in some stuff Well, and I'll do it and you'll see my actual process, but it's really just like that It's just a matter of taking my core patterns and I feel what sounds best What's first one second third and I essentially use the rules of the straight line?
